summary: Scarlet>Fire marathon and tight first set make this Uptown gem
blurb: The concert opens strong with classics like Friend Of The Devil, Cassidy and New Minglewood Blues before launching into a 45‑minute Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World suite that many fans call the highlight. Jerry’s soaring lead tone and Brent’s fire‑hot keyboards drive the jams, while the second set delivers a powerful Not Fade Away → Wharf Rat transition.
review: Chicago’s Uptown Theater on February 27, 1981 captured the Dead at a peak of chemistry. The first set rolls through fan favorites—Alabama Getaway, The Promised Land, Friend Of The Devil, Cassidy, Loser and New Minglewood Blues—with tight playing that sets the stage for what follows. The second set is where the show truly shines: a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue blossoms into an extended Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World suite lasting nearly forty‑five minutes, punctuated by a propulsive drum/space segment and a thunderous Not Fade Away → Wharf Rat climax that segues into Sugar Magnolia. Jerry Garcia delivers soaring lead tones and vocal performances (with only occasional lyric flubs), while Brent Mydland’s keyboard work—especially his Fender Rhodes intro and backing vocals—adds fire‑hot color throughout. The rhythm section stays solid; Phil’s bass anchors the groove even when it gets a bit buried in the mix, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann provide steady, propulsive drumming that fuels the jams. Overall, the concert is a classic Uptown night, praised for its energy, tightness, and one of the most memorable Scarlet>Fire sequences of the early‑’80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five of six recordings; consistent praise for the Scarlet Begonias → Fire → Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World suite, Brent's keyboard fire‑hot playing, and Jerry's soaring vocals. Only one mixed audience tape offered no standout moments, but it does not outweigh the overwhelming acclaim.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with flawless mix, full set, and the marquee Scarlet>Fire suite presented in pristine detail.

key_highlights: 45‑minute Scarlet Begonias → Fire → Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World marathon that defines the show.
Powerful Not Fade Away → Wharf Rat transition driving the second‑set climax.
Brent Mydland’s fire‑hot keyboard work and vocal harmonies throughout.
Tight first‑set classics (Friend Of The Devil, Cassidy, New Minglewood Blues) set a high bar.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring lead tone and strong vocal delivery, especially magical during the Scarlet>Fire transition; occasional lyric flubs noted but overall praised.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation throughout; a few listeners mention it gets buried in the mix on the soundboard recording.
band_performance.Bob: Provides occasional colorful bursts; some moments barely audible, but his freak‑out accents are appreciated when heard.
band_performance.Brent: Fire‑hot keyboard playing with a standout Fender Rhodes intro, tight comping and strong backing vocals that lift the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Propulsive drumming that drives the second‑set energy, especially during the drum/space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Steady, powerful rhythms complement Mickey; his work shines in the Not Fade Away → Wharf Rat transition.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
